summary.intent: Fix three Codex-identified edge case bugs in the image cropper component as part of an iterative multi-pass review cycle (round 4).
summary.outcome: All three fixes implemented with failing tests written first, 88 tests passing, clippy clean, spec synced, and changes committed/pushed. A fifth Codex pass was re-triggered.

repo: The image cropper lives in crates/ars-components/src/specialized/image_cropper/mod.rs with a paired spec at spec/components/specialized/image-cropper.md that must be kept in sync with implementation changes.
The project uses a `cargo xci-fast` command as the integration check gate before pushing.
Codex review is done in iterative passes; each pass surfaces new findings that are addressed in sequence, with threads replied+resolved on the PR after each round.
The feasible aspect ratio band is [0.05, 20] (i.e., [MIN, 1/MIN] where MIN=0.05), outside which ratios are treated as unconstrained to avoid sub-5% dimension collapse.

code.finding: as_ratio must reject ratios outside [0.05, 20] — extreme Fixed ratios like 100:1 can geometrically collapse a dimension below the 5% floor in a unit square.
constrain_to_ratio must reposition the crop origin (not just clamp size) so feasible ratios keep the minimum-size floor achievable near container edges.
on_root_keydown root shortcuts (R=reload, +=zoom) must gate on no ctrl/alt/meta modifiers to avoid intercepting browser/OS shortcuts like Ctrl+R and Cmd++. Shift is still allowed.
The transition function must drop DragStart/Move, ResizeStart/Move, and NudgeCrop events with non-finite coordinate values — the clamp operation does not sanitize NaN/Inf inputs.

workflow: TDD discipline maintained: failing tests written before each fix, verified to fail, then implementation added.
Coverage is checked after each round to ensure new branches are exercised.
Spec document is updated in the same commit as the implementation to keep them in sync.
Background tasks are used for slow operations (Codex poll, xci-fast) so work can continue while they run.

friction: constrain_to_ratio fix required multiple read/find iterations to locate the correct insertion point, suggesting the function is large or not trivially navigable.
Non-finite coordinate guard required finding the disabled gate location via grep before editing, indicating the transition function has non-obvious structure.
open_items: Fifth Codex pass has been re-triggered — further findings from that pass are not yet addressed.
PR threads for the three round-4 findings need to be replied+resolved (noted as next step after push).
